title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
A higher-order function (HOF) is a function that either:
1. Takes one or more functions as arguments, or
2. Returns a function.

This is possible because JavaScript treats functions as first-class citizens — they can be stored in variables, passed around, and returned like any other value.

Higher-order functions are the foundation of functional programming in JS. You use them every day: map, filter, reduce, addEventListener, setTimeout, and any function that accepts a callback.
